items.title: These are the countries moving to ban social media for children
items.description: Australia was the first country to issue a ban in late 2025, aiming to reduce the pressures and risks that young users may face on social media, including cyberbullying, social media addiction, and exposure to predators.

items.title: British cryptographer Adam Back denies NYT report that he is Bitcoin creator Satoshi Nakamoto
items.description: Back, a longtime researcher and pioneer in cryptography, denies the allegation that he is the secretive creator of Bitcoin.

items.title: Astropad’s Workbench reimagines remote desktop for AI agents, not IT support
items.description: Astropad’s Workbench lets users remotely monitor and control AI agents on Mac Minis from iPhone or iPad, with low-latency streaming and mobile access.

items.title: Hackers steal and leak sensitive LAPD police documents
items.description: The LAPD said the breach affected “a digital storage system” belonging to the city’s Attorney's Office. The World Leaks extortion gang was reported to be behind the attack.

items.title: OpenAI releases a new safety blueprint to address the rise in child sexual exploitation
items.description: OpenAI's new Child Safety Blueprint aims to tackle the alarming rise in child sexual exploitation linked to advancements in AI.

items.title: Developer of VeraCrypt encryption software says Windows users may face boot-up issues after Microsoft locked his account
items.description: The maker of the popular open source file encryption software VeraCrypt said Microsoft locked his online account, which may prevent device owners from booting up their computers.

items.title: Databricks co-founder wins prestigious ACM award, says ‘AGI is here already’
items.description: Matei Zaharia has won the top honor from the Association for Computing Machinery. Now he's working on AI for research and says AGI is simply misunderstood.

items.title: As YouTube grows on TV, it eyes more interactive video across formats

items.title: VC Eclipse has a new $1.3B fund to back — and build — ‘physical AI’ startups
items.description: Eclipse will put some of that money towards incubating, or building startups.

items.title: Iranian hackers are targeting American critical infrastructure, US agencies warn
items.description: A joint FBI, NSA, and CISA advisory warns that Iranian hackers have "escalated" their tactics in response to the ongoing U.S.-Israel war with Iran.

items.title: Firmus, the ‘Southgate’ AI data center builder backed by Nvidia, hits $5.5B valuation
items.description: Nvidia-backed Asia AI data center provider Firmus has now raised $1.35 billion in six months.

items.title: Intel signs on to Elon Musk’s Terafab chips project
items.description: Intel will join SpaceX and Tesla in an effort to build a new U.S. semiconductor factory in Texas, although the scope of its contributions are unclear.

items.title: Anthropic debuts preview of powerful new AI model Mythos in new cybersecurity initiative
items.description: The new model will be used by a small number of high-profile companies to engage in defensive cybersecurity work.

items.description: The news follows a report from Nikkei Asia on Tuesday that raised concerns the company’s foldable iPhone could be delayed due to challenges during the phone’s engineering test phase.
